note: Views Custom Cache Tag ships one Views cache plugin, `custom_tag` ("Custom Tag based", class Drupal\views_custom_cache_tag\Plugin\views\cache\CustomTag), selected per view display; it attaches user-supplied cache tags to a view and strips the entity *_list tags so invalidation is manual. Easy cases are graded on response text; medium cases install a known view (custom_tag cache + known tags) to read back and delete it after; hard cases build/switch a view's display cache option verified against live state by exit code. The project's bundled views_custom_cache_tag_demo is a demo only and is not documented as a submodule.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which contrib module lets me set my own cache tags on a View, and what is the machine id of the Views cache plugin it provides?
evals.expected_output: Views Custom Cache Tag (views_custom_cache_tag). It provides the Views cache plugin id `custom_tag` ("Custom Tag based"), selected under a view display's Advanced -> Caching.

evals.prompt: Using Views Custom Cache Tag, where do I enter the custom cache tags for a view and in what format? Does the module have its own admin settings page?
evals.expected_output: There is no separate admin/settings page or configure route. You set it per display: edit the view, open Advanced -> Other -> Caching, choose 'Custom Tag based' (custom_tag), and enter one cache tag per line in the 'Custom tag list' textarea.

evals.prompt: Why does Views Custom Cache Tag stop a view from being flushed on every node save, and how do I actually clear a view that uses it?
evals.expected_output: Its getCacheTags() removes the entity list cache tags (e.g. node_list) that core's Tag plugin would add, so unrelated saves no longer invalidate the view. You clear it yourself from code by invalidating your configured tag, e.g. Cache::invalidateTags(['my_tag']).

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a View named vcct_eval_tags that caches with Views Custom Cache Tag. Inspect the running site (drush available) and tell me the exact custom cache tags configured on its default display.
evals.expected_output: The vcct_eval_tags view's default display uses the custom_tag cache plugin with two custom cache tags: eval:report and eval:widgets (read from views.view.vcct_eval_tags display.default.display_options.cache.options.custom_tag).

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has a View named vcct_eval_plugin. Inspect the running site (drush available) and tell me which Views cache plugin its default display uses (give the plugin machine id).
evals.expected_output: The vcct_eval_plugin view's default display cache type is custom_tag (the Views Custom Cache Tag plugin), configured with the custom tag eval:catalog and a 3600-second rendered-output lifespan.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (DDEV, drush available), the Views Custom Cache Tag module is enabled. Create a new View with machine name exactly `vcct_hard_report` (a listing of content) and set its default display's cache plugin to Views Custom Cache Tag (the custom_tag plugin) with the custom cache tag `eval:hard:report`. Apply it to the running site with drush/config — do not just describe it. Summarize in one line what you created.
evals.expected_output: A view vcct_hard_report exists whose default display cache option is type custom_tag with a custom_tag list containing eval:hard:report. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), there is an existing View named vcct_hard_switch that currently uses the core Tag cache plugin. Switch its default display to use the Views Custom Cache Tag plugin (custom_tag) instead, with the custom cache tag `eval:hard:switch`. Apply the change to the running site — do not just describe it. State in one line what you changed.
evals.expected_output: The vcct_hard_switch view's default display cache option is now type custom_tag with a custom_tag list containing eval:hard:switch (changed from the core tag plugin). Verified against live site state.
